commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This needs maintainer API/security-boundary approval, a rebase, and contributor real behavior proof rather than an automated repair because the permanent contract between actions and externalResolution is a produ...; Needs attention: The diff introduces a new admin-scoped approval resolver, so the main security question is maintainer approval of that trust boundary rather than supply-chain risk.; Review findi... (sha=5e5de9d9f62511ef866a130f1e57aa437444a069)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an externalResolution plugin approval verification path, a plugin.approval.resolveVerified admin Gateway method, rendering/parsing support across approval surfaces, docs, generated protocol models, and tests.

PR surface: Source +620, Tests +741, Docs +50, Generated 0, Other +26. Total +1437 across 39 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows current main carries plugin approval actions while the PR branch replaces that payload shape with externalResolution. No runtime test was run because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer API/security-boundary approval, a rebase, and contributor real behavior proof rather than an automated repair because the permanent contract between actions and externalResolution is a product/API decision.

target.title: feat: add external plugin approval verification
target.body:
## Summary

This is the narrowed AgentKit host API slice after Kevin's feedback about avoiding a broad custom-action surface.

- Adds `externalResolution` for plugin approvals as a single command-template field, not arbitrary custom action arrays.
- Keeps core as the normal approval owner: OpenClaw still renders core decisions from `allowedDecisions`, and AgentKit uses only core `deny` there.
- Adds `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` for proof-backed allow resolution, scoped to the originating `pluginId` and registered behind `operator.admin`.
- Teaches approval view models, text/channel renderers, Control UI parsing, protocol schema, docs, and SDK types to carry/render the narrow external verification command.
- Rejects mixed normal allow decisions with external verification, so allow-once/allow-always must go through the verified plugin-owned resolver.

This replaces the earlier broader approach from #82431. The chat/status-card follow-up from #82471 is not required for the current AgentKit end-to-end path.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: external HITL plugins can ask OpenClaw to show a narrow verification command for allow decisions while core keeps denial and pending approval ownership.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 using the gateway/plugin approval tests, channel renderer tests, Control UI tests, full build, SDK API check, and autoreview against `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the commands listed in `Verification`.

Evidence after fix: focused approval suite passed with 15 files / 402 tests; the reaction-runtime regression test passed with 1 file / 12 tests; full build completed; SDK API baseline check passed; `git diff --check` passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: plugin approval prompts render external verification as command text, core deny remains available, verified allow resolution is admin-scoped and plugin-owned, and reaction/manual fallback prompts no longer claim `allow-always` is unavailable when external verification exposes it.

What was not tested: a live World App QR scan from the TUI in this OpenClaw PR branch. The companion AgentKit plugin branch covers the plugin-level HITL and local OpenClaw gateway e2e path.
target.author: guardiola31337
